description:
The SETroubleshoot plugins are used to analyze SELinux AVC data. The service provides information around configuration errors, unauthorized intrusions, and other potential errors. The setroubleshoot-plugins package can be removed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf remove setroubleshoot-plugins
rationale:
The SETroubleshoot service is an unnecessary daemon to
have running on a server.
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the setroubleshoot-plugins package is installed: 
$ rpm -q setroubleshoot-plugins
title: Uninstall setroubleshoot-plugins Package
